bio.name: Sugat Dhawane
bio.title: Frontend Lead Engineer
bio.summary: Frontend Lead with 15+ years building enterprise-grade financial applications for PNC Bank and Charles Schwab. Deep expertise in Angular (v18+), React.js, TypeScript, and Micro-frontend Architecture, with a proven record of delivering scalable, accessible, and high-performance web applications in regulated fintech environments. Experienced in design system ownership, CI/CD automation, and cross-functional collaboration from requirements through production deployments.

highlights: Led frontend architecture for a Loan Processing Application, defining UI standards and driving technical decisions across a team of 5+ engineers.
Designed and delivered a Loan Processing Application using Angular, TypeScript, RxJS, and Kendo UI, reducing average loan processing time significantly.
Implemented Micro Frontend Architecture using Webpack's Module Federation across 7 independently deployable Angular projects, cutting release cycle time by ~40%.
Integrated REST APIs from backend .NET services to connect data and streamline workflow across loan processing modules.
Achieved 80% unit test coverage (Jasmine/Karma) and integrated SonarQube quality gates into CI/CD pipeline to automatically block regression.
Mentored 4 junior and mid-level developers in Angular, TypeScript, and testing best practices, reducing PR rework cycles by ~25%.
Built and maintained 20+ reusable Web Components using Stencil JS, Angular, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, enabling consistent UI patterns across product teams.
Contributed to the Schwab Design Library, delivering a component set that standardized accessibility implementation (WCAG 2.1).
Partnered with UX designers in Figma to translate wireframes into responsive, accessible components for the Statement and Reports project.
Developed React.js components for internal tooling, focusing on component modularity and backend API integration.
Received Summit Individual Awards twice for rapidly delivering proof-of-concepts alongside regular project commitments.
Led creation of responsive web pages from initial drafts to final implementation using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Ensured adherence to UX principles and WCAG accessibility standards.
Managed UI aspects of projects from design through ongoing maintenance and support.
Developed key features for an e-commerce platform using Angular, HTML, CSS, and Bootstrap.
Resolved cross-browser compatibility issues ensuring seamless functionality across all major browsers.
Delivered new features using ExtJS within a 3-week ramp-up period, meeting project deadlines.
Gained foundational experience in Web Development, PHP, and Software Delivery across three Mumbai-based firms.

projects.name: Loan Processing Application
projects.description: Enterprise-grade loan processing platform handling end-to-end loan workflows for one of the largest US banks. Led frontend architecture and technical decisions for a team of 5+ engineers.
highlights: Micro Frontend Architecture with Module Federation
7 independently deployable Angular projects
40% reduction in release cycle time
80% unit test coverage

projects.name: Schwab Design Library & Web Components
projects.description: Built and maintained 20+ reusable Web Components as part of Charles Schwab's design system, enabling consistent UI patterns and accessibility standards across multiple product teams.
highlights: 20+ reusable components
Standardized WCAG 2.1 accessibility
Accelerated UI development across teams

projects.name: Statement and Reports UI
projects.description: Responsive, accessible UI for brokerage statement and reports, serving Charles Schwab's retail brokerage users. Translated Figma wireframes into production-ready components.
highlights: Accessible brokerage UI
Figma-to-code implementation
JAWS screen reader support

projects.name: E-commerce Platform
projects.description: Developed seller information pages and key features for an e-commerce platform with cross-browser compatibility.
highlights: Cross-browser compatibility
Delivered ExtJS features in 3-week ramp-up

awards.title: Summit Individual Award
awards.reason: Rapidly delivering proof-of-concepts alongside regular project commitments, demonstrating high productivity under competing priorities.
